meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/purview) — portal path, banner wording, and PowerShell grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-03.
generalNotes: Audit logging is ON by default for Microsoft 365 / Office 365 enterprise tenants — but NOT for Business Premium / SMB licenses or unmanaged trial tenants, where it must be turned on manually. Since these baselines target M365 Business Premium, verify rather than assume.
You need the Audit Logs role in Exchange Online (the Organization Management or Compliance Management role groups have it) to turn auditing on or off.
The 'Start recording user and admin activity' banner only appears when auditing is OFF — if you don't see it, auditing is already on. Confirm either way with the Get-AdminAuditLogConfig check below.

requiredSettings.label: UnifiedAuditLogIngestionEnabled
requiredSettings.value: True (unified audit log recording turned on)

requiredSettings.label: Default retention
requiredSettings.value: 180 days for Audit (Standard); longer with per-user audit retention policies / higher licenses

requiredSettings.label: Propagation
requiredSettings.value: Can take up to ~60 minutes after enabling before events are searchable

steps: Sign in to the Microsoft Purview portal (purview.microsoft.com) with an account that has the Audit Logs role.
Open the Audit solution card (if it isn't shown, select View all solutions → Audit under Core).
If auditing is off, a banner 'Start recording user and admin activity' appears — select it. (No banner = already on.)
Alternatively, enable it from Exchange Online PowerShell: Set-AdminAuditLogConfig -UnifiedAuditLogIngestionEnabled $true

Verify (run in Exchange Online PowerShell, NOT Security & Compliance PowerShell, where the value always reads False): Get-AdminAuditLogConfig | Format-List UnifiedAuditLogIngestionEnabled — expect True.

Note: this is invisible to end users — no functional change. It's the prerequisite for the Office 365 Management Activity API, Microsoft Sentinel ingestion, and any audit-log search / incident investigation.
